reasoning: The root cause was identified as severe packet loss on the F1-c interface (downlink), which disrupted control plane signaling between the O-CU and O-DU, causing a cascading failure across the RAN. This was prioritized over volumetric and latency symptoms due to the 'hard error' nature of packet loss.
actionable_steps: 1. Inspect the transport network link between the O-CU and O-DU for physical faults or congestion. 2. Verify the F1-c interface configurations on the O-CU and O-DU for MTU mismatches or protocol errors. 3. Check for recent software updates or policy changes to O-CU/O-DU that may have introduced instability in control plane traffic.
